Database service disruption affecting vector search.
Database connection pooling, automated restarts, and regular backup routines.

sheet.p: Business-related risks should also be considered.

jfield.val: Incorrect or unverified answers impacting user confidence.
RAG-based grounding, clear website source links, and conservative confidence thresholds.
Unpredictable API costs under high usage.
Token tracking, prompt optimization, and optional rate limiting.

sheet.p: Risks should be reviewed periodically during development and after production deployment.
The review process involves the following sequential checks:

steps.step: Review API costs, token usage, and latency.
Analyze thumbs up/down and explicit user feedback.
Identify failed, unanswered, or low-confidence queries.
Assess the accuracy and hallucination rate of AI responses.
Expand knowledge base coverage to address gaps.

sheet.p: Continuous review allows the chatbot to evolve while maintaining reliability and accuracy.

row.cell: Minimizes hallucinations by grounding responses in approved content.
Ensures knowledge remains current without unnecessary processing.
Enables early detection of technical issues.
Reduces dependency on a single AI provider and improves future flexibility.

sheet.label: THETA CHATBOT · ROADMAP
sheet.title: Chapter 16 – Future Roadmap
sheet.p: The Theta Chat Bot has been designed using a modular and extensible architecture that supports future enhancements with minimal changes to the existing system. While the initial implementation focuses on delivering accurate website-based responses using a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) architecture, the proposed design enables the chatbot to evolve incrementally as business requirements grow.
The roadmap below outlines the planned evolution of the chatbot while maintaining scalability, maintainability, and deployment flexibility.
The first phase focuses on delivering a production-ready chatbot integrated with the Theta Technolabs website.

row.cell: Website-based knowledge retrieval utilizing RAG architecture.
Google Gemini API integration (with OpenAI as a tested alternative).
Sentence Transformers for generating local vector embeddings.
PostgreSQL integration with pgvector for vector search.
Webflow chatbot integration with session-based conversation memory.
Source-aware responses and confidence-based fallback handling.

sheet.label: THETA CHATBOT · ROADMAP
sheet.p: Following the initial deployment, the focus shifts toward improving operational visibility, monitoring, and performance analysis.

row.cell: Centralized interface for overall system visibility and management.
Comprehensive request, response, and conversation history logging.
Performance monitoring and continuous usage analytics tracking.
Knowledge synchronization history and automated audit trails.
AI-powered conversation summaries and direct user feedback collection.
Implementation of CAPTCHA and API rate-limiting.

sheet.p: Objective: Improve operational monitoring and provide actionable insights into chatbot usage, conversation trends, and overall system performance.
This phase focuses on enhancing the overall user experience and making interactions more engaging and accessible.

row.cell: Voice-based interaction and file upload support.
Rich response cards and dynamically suggested follow-up questions.
Multi-language support for diverse and global user bases.
Enhanced conversation memory mapping for long-running contexts.
Continued and targeted accessibility improvements for all users.

sheet.label: THETA CHATBOT · ROADMAP
sheet.p: The long-term vision is to continuously enhance the Theta Chat Bot by improving response quality, user experience, and operational efficiency. Future enhancements will focus on increasing system performance, simplifying maintenance, and supporting evolving business requirements without requiring significant architectural changes.

row.cell: Multi-website support and continuous backend performance optimization.
Improved RAG techniques and enhanced semantic search accuracy.
Advanced conversation analytics and fully automated reporting.
Improved algorithmic response quality evaluation processes.

sheet.p: This design provides the flexibility to incorporate these enhancements incrementally while maintaining system stability.

row.cell: Deploy a production-ready RAG chatbot and establish the core AI platform
Introduce monitoring, dashboards, and operational analytics
Expand the chatbot through continuous improvements and support for future business requirements

sheet.p: The phased roadmap enables incremental development while minimizing implementation risk. Each phase builds upon the existing foundation, allowing the Theta Chat Bot to evolve into a scalable, intelligent customer support solution without requiring a complete system redesign.

sheet.label: THETA CHATBOT · APPENDIX
sheet.title: Appendix A – LLM Cost Comparison
sheet.p: This appendix provides a comprehensive financial and operational cost breakdown for Large Language Model (LLM) providers and local embedding models evaluated for the Theta Chat Bot.
The table below details current market pricing (USD per 1 Million tokens) for the primary and fallback LLM candidate models.

row.cell: Default choice for high-speed RAG Q&A

sheet.p: Assuming an average user interaction consists of 2,000 input tokens (retrieved website context + prompt + question) and generates a 500-token response:
sheet.callout: At this rate, 1,000 complete customer conversations cost approximately $1.25 USD (₹120.41 INR) in AI API fees, ensuring extremely low operational expenses.

row.cell: 100% savings on embedding API fees. Sentence Transformers runs locally on internal servers with zero per-token costs.
Caching answers for common questions (e.g., business hours, location, core services) reduces LLM API calls by 30–40%.
Running Qdrant or PostgreSQL + pgvector in Docker containers avoids monthly third-party cloud vector DB subscriptions.
Filtering out low-relevance chunks ensures context stays under 2,000 tokens, directly lowering input token billing.

sheet.label: THETA CHATBOT · APPENDIX
sheet.title: Requirement Gathering Questions

p.b: Q1: Which website(s) should the chatbot support (e.g., only thetatechnolabs.in or multiple domains such as .com and .ca)?

sheet.why: This determines the overall scope of the chatbot's knowledge base and helps define how the data should be organized and stored. Supporting multiple websites may require separate knowledge collections, metadata management, or a scalable storage architecture to efficiently manage content from different domains.

p.b: Q1: How frequently is the website content updated (e.g., service pages, portfolios, case studies)?

sheet.why: The frequency of website updates helps determine how often the knowledge base should be refreshed to ensure users receive accurate and up-to-date information.

p.b: Q2: How often are new blogs or articles published?

sheet.why: Blogs are often updated independently of core website pages. Understanding their publishing frequency helps define an appropriate synchronization schedule for blog content.

p.b: Q3: Will someone notify the team about content updates, or can we automate the knowledge base update process using the existing website CMS?

sheet.why: This helps determine whether the chatbot's knowledge base should rely on manual updates or whether it can be automatically synchronized whenever content is published or modified through the existing CMS. If CMS automation is available, it can reduce manual effort, keep the knowledge base synchronized with the latest website content, and minimize ongoing maintenance.

sheet.label: THETA CHATBOT · APPENDIX

p.b: Q1: The existing website already has a manual inquiry chat widget on the right side of the website. Are conversation details such as user queries, responses, timestamps, and other interaction data currently being stored? If yes, where is this data stored, and can I access it?

sheet.why: Understanding whether the existing inquiry chat stores conversation data helps identify if valuable historical information is already available. Access to this data can provide insights into the most frequently asked questions, common user inquiries, recurring issues, and user behavior. It also helps determine whether the existing chat platform can be used as a source for analytics, reporting, and improving the new chatbot's knowledge base, while understanding where the data is stored (e.g., database, CRM, third-party service, or CMS) helps assess integration and migration possibilities.

p.b: Q1: Who is the primary audience for the chatbot (e.g., potential clients, existing clients, job seekers, or general website visitors)?

sheet.why: Understanding the target audience helps define the chatbot's communication style, response depth, supported use cases, and the type of information that should be prioritized.

sheet.label: THETA CHATBOT · APPENDIX

p.b: Q1: Should the chatbot be available on every page of the website or only on selected pages?

sheet.why: The chatbot's placement affects user accessibility, frontend integration, user experience, and whether context-specific behavior is required for different sections of the website.

p.b: Q1: Does the existing server have sufficient CPU, RAM, and storage to host the chatbot and its supporting services?

sheet.why: This helps determine whether the existing infrastructure is capable of hosting the chatbot and its supporting services. If the available resources are insufficient, cloud deployment or additional infrastructure planning may be required to ensure reliable performance and future scalability.

sheet.label: THETA CHATBOT · APPENDIX

p.b: Q1: Is an admin dashboard required for managing the knowledge base, chatbot configuration, or analytics?

sheet.why: This helps identify the required administrative capabilities and provides a better understanding of future system requirements. It also helps determine the most suitable database architecture (for example, PostgreSQL with pgvector or a dedicated vector database) based on future needs such as analytics, conversation history, reporting, and scalability.

p.b: Q1: Is there a monthly budget limit for AI API usage?

sheet.why: Understanding the available budget influences the selection of AI models, response generation strategy, token usage optimization, and long-term operational cost planning.
